title: The Road West
subtitle: Village of Barovia · Svalich Road · The Gates of Ravenloft
opener: The mist doesn't ask. It takes what it takes, returns what it returns, and the group learned long ago that looking for logic in it is a waste of effort. Kaelos and Dara disappeared into the bank of fog. Thokk emerged from it. Barovia made an exchange nobody authorised, and the only possible response was to keep walking.

sections.heading: The Farewell and the Swords
paragraphs: Ismark was waiting at the mansion threshold. He had two silver swords — one for Sarda, one for Thokk. He handed them over without ceremony, like someone settling a debt before the other party changes their mind. Ireena was already ready: small bag, dark cloak, the expression of someone who has made a decision and does not intend to revisit it.
The farewell was what it was. Ismark raised a hand as the cart moved off. The village windows were shut as always. A curtain shifted somewhere. Nobody came out.

sections.heading: The Mist — What Left and What Came Back
paragraphs: Shortly after leaving the village, a dense bank of mist closed across the road. Kaelos and Dara disappeared into it. From that same bank emerged Thokk, a knife in his hand and the expression of someone who has just arrived from somewhere but doesn't know where.

Barovia offered no explanation. It never does. The group absorbed the exchange with the resignation of people who have been in the valley long enough to know that asking for answers is an expenditure of energy they cannot afford.

sections.heading: The Bridge
paragraphs: A couple of hours down the road, a stone bridge crossed a serious river — wide, running hard, with a drop of about ten metres from the deck to the water below. The river ran west to east, from the direction of the castle toward the village, fast and cold. The mist rising off it was wet and freezing. They crossed without stopping.

sections.heading: The Tree and the Hanged Man
paragraphs: Hours past the bridge, on the right side of the road: a massive and very old tree, leafless, with nearly horizontal branches twisted together. A man was hanging from one of them. Traveller's clothes — someone from outside, like them.

Part of the group climbed down from the cart to investigate. The man had been dead for days, perhaps a week. Enna handled cutting the body down. In the inner pocket of his cloak was an unfinished letter addressed to someone named Petra, in a place that does not exist inside Barovia. The text mentioned the river gypsies — friendly, with warm food, who ask no questions. And who always look toward the castle. The letter did not finish. The last line was never written. On his wrist, a clean bandage made from brightly coloured cloth.

Five zombies and a Wight emerged from the forest while the group's attention was on the body. The tree was the decoy. The fight was on the road, with the cart at their backs and the forest closing in on the flanks. When the Wight fell, the last zombie fleeing from Laucian's holy symbol dropped dead where it stood.

sections.heading: Strahd's Carriage
paragraphs: At the crossroads toward Ravenloft, a black carriage waited motionless in the road. Black throughout — wood, roof, curtains. Drawn by two horses. Sarda opened her divine sense before approaching: the horses were ordinary animals, no trace of what should not still be alive.

The carriage door opened on its own as the group drew near. Inside: darkness and silence. The group debated. Nobody got in. They left the carriage at the crossroads and continued west along the main road.

sections.heading: The Gates of Ravenloft — The Letter
paragraphs: The gates giving access to the road toward Ravenloft stood open. The same statues as the valley's entrance gates, somewhat better preserved. Sheltering from the damp beneath the arch, an oil lantern in hand: a hunched, hooded figure. Sarda stepped forward. Who are you? A servant, miss. Whose? Of master Strahd.

The figure handed over a letter and began walking toward the castle without adding anything further. The letter, written in the precise hand of someone who has had centuries to practise, was a dinner invitation to Ravenloft in three days' time. Strahd introduced himself as lord of Barovia and host, acknowledged the group as his subjects, and promised safe passage to the castle and civilised treatment at his table. Signed: Strahd von Zarovich, Count of Barovia.
The group read the letter. Discussed its implications. Sarda kept it.

sections.heading: The Rest
paragraphs: They camped near the gates, on the logic that Strahd's letter was also a kind of shield — nothing under his dominion would touch his invited guests that night. The logic was debatable. It worked. The night passed without incident. In the morning, Vallaki was still to the west and the castle was still to the north, and the group had three days to decide what to do with the invitation.

acquired: Letter from Strahd von Zarovich — dinner invitation to Ravenloft in three days (Sarda)
known: Thokk emerged from the mist where Kaelos and Dara disappeared — Barovia made an exchange with no explanation
The hanged man was an outsider adventurer — his letter mentions the river gypsies always looking toward the castle
The mark on the hanged man's wrist is a vampire bite, bandaged with Vistani cloth
Strahd's carriage was waiting at the Ravenloft crossroads — the horses were ordinary, the door opened on its own
Strahd has invited the group to dinner in three days — he promises safe passage and civilised treatment
The Vistani camp was not visited — the group passed by to avoid stopping with Ireena
unanswered: Who exactly was the hanged adventurer, and how long had he been in Barovia?
What does it mean to accept — or refuse — the dinner invitation?
What does the Vistani camp know about the group — Alenka had time to report before they reached the crossroads?
people: Ireena Kolyana — travelling with the group, quiet, watchful
Ismark — stayed in the village, watched them leave
Strahd's servant — hooded figure, delivered the letter, said nothing more
sidebar.milestone: The group holds an invitation from Strahd. Three days. Vallaki remains the immediate destination.
